observations.recommendation: The Management to file a request for approval of COA to write-off from the books the dormant inter-agency receivables amounting to ₱0.404 million pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

The Management require the concerned personnel to comply with pertinent regulations in relation to the repair and maintenance of equipment and vehicles, particularly on the attachment of complete documentary requirements to ensure that proper procedure is followed; and

The Municipal Accountant cautiously check the completeness of all the pertinent supporting documents before allowing the payment, otherwise the transaction/s will be suspended in audit.

1.13  We recommended and Management agreed to require the Accountant to:
Review, analyze and reclassify the CIP to the appropriate PPE accounts and make the necessary adjustment on the Accumulated Depreciation and Accumulated Surplus/(Deficit) accounts; and
Start preparing the detailed lapsing schedule of PPE accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Budget Office and Municipal Accounting Office coordinate in the determination of charging of repairs and maintenance so as to avoid misclassifications, taking into consideration the change in estimated useful life of the assets as a result of the major repairs.

7.10   We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Ensure that the amounts deducted from the Gross Pay should reconcile with the amounts remitted as summarized in the Remittance List which should always be attached to the Disbursement Voucher to be submitted for post-audit;
Regularly transfer the cash from MEED Fund to the General Fund for the equivalent amount deducted from MEED personnel; and
Analyze and reconcile the noted discrepancy of P48,310.53 between the SLs and GL and take up necessary correcting entries.
Submit corrected SLs.

10.9  We recommended and Management agreed to:
Pursue the same commitment every year to implement its major PPAs and maintain efficient planning and procurement processes, strict monitoring and supervision of the engineering aspects of construction projects; and
The Municipal Engineer require the Contractor to work on the noted deficiencies or to refund the amounts, if necessary. Submit pictures, Official Receipts upon compliance.

11.4  We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The GAD Focal Point System should prioritize the formulation and adoption of its Gender and Development Agenda in accordance with the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. The GAD Agenda should cover the current three-year term of the LGU leadership and serve as the strategic basis for the annual GAD Plan and Budget; and
The GFPS Technical Working Group (TWG) must also ensure that the GAD Agenda is reviewed and endorsed by the Local Development Council and approved by the Municipal Mayor to ensure institutional support, proper alignment of programs and budgets, and compliance with national gender mainstreaming policies.

11.8 We recommended and Management agreed to create or assign a Responsibility Center for GAD Focal Point System under the Office of the Municipal Mayor and reflect the assigned RC Code in the computerized or manual accounting system of the LGU.

11.14   We recommended and Management agreed to require the:
GAD Focal Point System prepare and submit detailed schedules of GAD expenditures to expedite the tracing of the transactions from the submitted disbursement vouchers; and
Municipal Accountant prepare and regularly update subsidiary ledgers for all GAD-related transactions.

The Municipal Accountant enforce daily the submission of all the reports and documents from the Municipal Treasurer. Then all submitted reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month to the Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under Sections 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

The Municipal Accountant and the MEED Officer reconcile both records and to submit revised schedule of receivables and/or take up adjusting entry, if warranted;

The Municipal Mayor take action to demand full collection of the ₱233,750.00 Accounts Receivable from the concerned Contractor to avoid possible loss of government funds due to its non-collectivity.

The Municipal Accountant and the MEED Officer reconcile both records and to submit revised schedule of receivables and/or take up adjusting entry, if warranted;

The Municipal Mayor take action to demand full collection of the ₱233,750.00 Accounts Receivable from the concerned Contractor to avoid possible loss of government funds due to its non-collectivity; and
The Municipal Accountant to take up adjusting entries to record the adjustments/ corrections.
The Accountant must observe the provisions of Section 111 of PD 1445 in the keeping of accounts.

Trace back prior years’ record and identify the employees with under or over remittance. Accordingly, remit immediately the unremitted contributions/loan payments or require the employee/s to pay in cash for the over remittance;

Submit the rest of the 2023 DVs and ensure that all future DVs be completely submitted for post audit.

The management to pursue the same commitment every year and maintain efficient planning and procurement process, strict monitoring and supervision of the engineering aspects of construction projects, at the same time work on the partially/unimplemented PPAs; and

Require the Contractor to install the urinal, otherwise submit Variation Order if the same was changed to other items or scope of work with pictures of the completed work/delivered item.

Maintain its GAD Plan integration in the LGU’s regular activities coupled with compliance with existing GAD laws, policies, and rules; and

Continue addressing gender issues by ensuring gender-responsive Programs, Projects and Activities.

The Municipal Accountant enforce daily the submission of all the reports and documents from the Municipal Treasurer. Then all submitted reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month to the Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under Sections 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Municipal Accountant and the MEED Officer reconcile both records and to submit revised schedule of receivables and/or take up adjusting entry, if warranted;
Municipal Mayor take action to demand full collection of the P233,750.00 Accounts Receivable from the concerned Contractor to avoid possible loss of government funds due to its non-collectivity; and
MEED Officer sends demand letters to collect past due water bills and consider disconnection of service if warranted.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain and update the schedules and aging of APs regularly complete with necessary details on the accounts and to ensure that the schedules are submitted on time;
Record the 10% retention money from Contracts as Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable; and
Take up correcting entry to adjust the P0.445 million and record the same as Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Record the liquidation of cash advances in accordance with the generally accepted accounting principles.  Record the liquidation up to the extent of cash advance and the excess as expense if reimbursement is requested;
Require the Accountable Officer/s who have existing under liquidation to immediately pay/refund the corresponding amount; and
Henceforth, demand from the Accountable Officer refund of the excess/unutilized potions of cash advance upon liquidation attaching the Official Receipt for the payment made.

We recommended and the Management agreed to file a request for approval of COA to write-off from the books the dormant inter-agency receivables amounting to P0.404 million pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the Designated General Services Officer towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the recording of all Inventory Accounts to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials;
The Municipal Accountant and the Designated General Services Officer maintain the prescribed ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, for each type of Inventory, keep track the actual Supplies Inventory on hand and reconcile the balances of the two inventory records; and
The Municipal Accountant to take up adjusting entries to record the adjustments/corrections.
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Accountant must observe the provisions of Section 111 of PD 1445 in the keeping of accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Management require the concerned personnel to comply with pertinent regulations in relation to the repair and maintenance of equipment and vehicles, particularly on the attachment of complete documentary requirements to ensure that proper procedure is followed; and
The Municipal Accountant cautiously check the completeness of all the pertinent supporting documents before allowing the payment, otherwise the transaction/s will be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Trace back prior years’ record and identify the employees with under or over remittance. Accordingly, remit immediately the unremitted contributions/loan payments or require the employee/s to pay in cash for the over remittance;
Ensure that the amounts deducted from the Gross Pay should reconcile with the amounts remitted as summarized in the Remittance List which should always be attached to the Disbursement Voucher to be submitted for post-audit; and
Submit the rest of the 2023 DVs and ensure that all future DVs be completely submitted for post audit.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Trace back prior years’ records and identify the employees with under or over remittance. Accordingly, remit immediately the unremitted contributions/loan payments or require the employee/s to pay in cash in case of over remittance; and
Make certain that the amounts deducted from the Gross Pay reconcile with the amounts remitted as summarized in the Remittance List which should always be attached to the Disbursement Voucher to be submitted for post-audit.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The management to pursue the same commitment every year and maintain efficient planning and procurement process, strict monitoring and supervision of the engineering aspects of construction projects, at the same time work on the partially/unimplemented PPAs; and
Require the Contractor to install the urinal, otherwise submit Variation Order if the same was changed to other items or scope of work with pictures of the completed work/delivered item.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipality’s GAD Focal Point Person:
Maintain its GAD Plan integration in the LGU’s regular activities coupled with compliance with existing GAD laws, policies, and rules; and
Continue addressing gender issues by ensuring gender-responsive Programs, Projects and Activities.

The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment to account for the incorrect recording of the P300,000.00 collection. Moreover, strengthen the review and analysis of transactions before recording to correctly identify the nature of the transactions and ensure the correctness of recording; and

The MEEDO Officer to continue updating and furnish monthly reports to the Accounting Office as basis for recording and for the Municipal Accountant to ensure that records are kept properly to support the balances reported so that the validity and accuracy could be ascertained.

The Municipal Mayor to issue the certification of absolute necessity for the availment of actual accommodation expenses that exceed the accommodation component of the DTE when the assigned task of the officials and employees necessitates the same.

We recommended to the management to refund the total amount of P684,000.00 paid to the facility owned by the husband of the Municipal Mayor for the rental of Ligtas Covid Center, thereby violating the Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public Officials and Employees for having pecuniary interest on government transactions.
observations.reason: Subject to issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended and the Management agreed to require:
The Accountant and the Designated Supply Officer to reconcile and analyze the difference between their respective records and effect the necessary adjustments;
The Designated Supply Officer to transfer all identified unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) Form as prescribed in Annex 51, Volume 2 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, for proper disposal and basis to derecognize from the books of accounts; and
Undertake the necessary actions for disposal in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and the rules and procedures provided for in COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.

It was recommended and the Management agreed to:
1.  	Demand   from the Contractor immediate rectification/repair of the structural defects noted on the concrete water reservoir and the Bel-is Road Concreting, and to install materials according to specifications, otherwise, enforce rights as provided for in Section 62 Rule XIX of the R-IRR of RA 9184;
Require the Engineering Unit to strictly monitor the implementation of the infra projects; and
3.  	Assign a team from the Job Order personnel doing utility works to regularly monitor and maintain the cleanliness around the enclosed premises of the El Progreso Water System.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to:
Stop the payment of regular and non-petty expenses thru reimbursement method;
Comply strictly with COA Circular No. 97-002 on the principle of imprest fund wherein regular, recurring and non-petty expenses are paid in checks in the name of the supplier/service provider; and
Make use of the petty cash fund in reimbursing small or petty expenses which cannot be conveniently paid thru check.
Submit a copy of the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing the payment of Load Allowance with list of qualified recipients;
Ensure that shopping, as alternative mode of procurement, be supported with the abstract of canvass and canvass from at least three suppliers; and
The Municipal Accountant should henceforth make sure that all DVs have attached JEVs and that all JEVs are completely filled up.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the:
a)  Municipal Accountant to verify the details of the accounts Due to NGAs. For completed projects, submit Report on Fund Utilization/Liquidation Report with complete supporting documents to the Source Agency and to refund excess amount, if any;
b)  Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts; and
c)  The Municipal Engineer to coordinate with AKELCO thru the Office of the Mayor. Submit/follow up previous request/s to hasten the resolution of low power supply in Sitio Hinugtan.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the Accounts Payable account and effect the necessary adjustment/s to revert the balances aged more than two years pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445;
Trace balances to identify the reconciling items, record adjustment/s and submit corrected/revised Schedules; and
Henceforth, regularly prepare and update Schedules of Accounts Payable to keep track of the list of all the outstanding accounts and ensure that the Schedule are reconciled with the balances reflected in the Financial Statement.

We recommended the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016 on the required analysis of dormant accounts; and
2.   Comply with all the conditions and requirements of the said Circular for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables from the Commission On Audit.
We recommended that the management implement the programmed/targeted projects and activities and close monitoring be made to make sure that these projects are implemented within the time frame.
observations.reason: Low percentage of utilization for CY 2020 and 2021 budget allocation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management submit to the Office of the Auditor the following: 

Original copy of the Delivery Receipts, Charged Sales Invoices and pictures of materials delivered for the Provision of Nego Cart.  Explain why the same were not attached to the Disbursement Vouchers.

We recommended that the Management:

Utilize the LDRRMF for its identified projects, programs or activities according to the schedules set in the Annual Investment Program.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant enforce daily the submission of all the reports and documents from the Municipal Treasurer. Then all submitted reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month  to the  Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under Sections 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Require the Inventory Committee complete the conduct of annual physical inventory of all PPE items, prepare the required the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and a copy of which be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of the following year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and

PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards for each group of PPEs shall be maintained by the Accounting Office and General Services Office, respectively.

We recommended and the management agreed to require Bids and Awards Committee strictly comply with the mandatory posting requirements of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 to promote transparency and accountability for all emergency procurements relative in the implementation of Bayanihan Act.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and cause the prompt submission of their monthly Consolidated RAAF together with the individual RAAF of Revenue Collectors to the Office of the Auditor not later that 5th day of the ensuing month. Also, see to it that all accountable forms with or without money value are properly accounted for in the report.

We recommended that the management submit to the Office of the Auditor the approval papers of the DOLE Regional Director of the above-mentioned deviation/change of the specifications of materials.

We recommended that the management submit to the Office of the Auditor the following documents such as:

Individual Beneficiary Profile (with picture) of the 105 beneficiaries of Negosyo sa Kariton;
Certifications from Municipal Social Welfare Office and Punong Barangays that the listed beneficiaries fall on the above eligibility requirements and/or that there are not a member of Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program (4Ps); and
Explain why upon validation, the recipient were not all informal workers as stated in the MOA.
We recommended that the management submit to the Office of the Auditor the following: 

Justification on non-distribution of basic sanitary tools and accessories to the beneficiaries; and
Justification on damaged/incompatible items upon distribution to beneficiaries.
We recommended that the management submit to the Office of the Auditor the following: 

Original copy of the Delivery Receipts, Charged Sales Invoices and pictures of materials delivered for the Provision of Nego Cart.  Explain why the same were not attached to the Disbursement Vouchers; 
Fund Utilization Report or Liquidation Report of Kabuhayan Program duly received by the DOLE Region; and
Proof of remittance of unutilized balance from the DOLE.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the Inventory Committee complete the conduct of annual physical inventory of all PPE items, prepare the required the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and a copy of which be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of the following year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and
PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards for each group of PPEs shall be maintained by the Accounting Office and General Services Office, respectively.

We recommended to the management to refund the total amount of P684,000.00 paid to the facility owned by the husband of the Municipal Mayor for the rental of Ligtas Covid Center, thereby violating the Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public Officials and Employees for having pecuniary interest on government transactions.

We recommended and the management agreed to require Bids and Awards Committee strictly comply with the mandatory posting requirements of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 to promote transparency and accountability for all emergency procurements relative in the implementation of Bayanihan Act.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and cause the prompt submission of their monthly Consolidated RAAF together with the individual RAAF of Revenue Collectors to the Office of the Auditor not later that 5th day of the ensuing month. Also, see to it that all accountable forms with or without money value are properly accounted for in the report.

We recommended that:

1. The inventory taking shall be done by the office and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE on the results thereof; and


2. The RPCPPE shall then be reconciled with accounting records in order to ensure that PPE account balances in the financial statements are fairly presented.
We recommended that the management implement the programmed/targeted projects and activities and close monitoring be made to make sure that these projects are implemented within the time frame.
We recommended that the management demand the supplier to register the purchased garbage truck with the LTO and insuring the same to GSIS TPL as stipulated in the P.O.

We recommended that the Management:

Utilize the LDRRMF for its identified projects, programs or activities according to the schedules set in the Annual Investment Program. 

Justify the delayed implementation of the identified projects, programs or activities.

Then all submitted reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month  to the  Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under Sections 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

2. Justify the delayed implementation of the identified projects, programs or activities.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections with the   AGDB, daily or not later than the next banking day. Likewise, reconcile with the accounting records the amount before the same should be deposited.

All submitted reports and supporting documents shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month to the Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under par. 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:

Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through the bank, as a means of improving fiscal management in accordance with Section 4(4) and (8) of PD 1445, with the expected result of minimizing the attendant risks identified with cash-paid payrolls and easing the effects of manpower attrition; 

Make a representation/ arrange with any government bank like the LBP for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by the employees through a Memorandum of Agreement; and

Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement to strengthen fiscal management.

We recommended that the LDRRMO and Accounting Office prioritize the preparation and submission of the reports on LDRRMF to aid stakeholders/management in performing their decision making functions. The reports should contain complete and relevant information.

We recommended that the Management:

Be realistic and economical in planning the work to be done  in the projects to be implemented to avoid the occurrence of the above mentioned deficiencies;

Follow strictly the documentary requirements as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and fill up all the required data properly; and

Refund the deficiencies in the total amount of P 26,739.97 and submit to the Office of the Audit Team the duly authenticated machine copy of the Official Receipt.

We recommended that the Management:

Modify/correct the defects immediately and submit the duly dated and authenticated pictures (before, during and after) to the office of the audit team;

Strictly follow the provisions of the Municipal Ordinance No. 21 series of 2013 in the implementation of project in the coastal zone and Sections 62.2 of the RIRR of RA 9184 in correcting the defects; and

Justify the above findings.

We recommend that the Local Government should conduct physical inventory of supplies semestrally and Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) annually and submit the required report thereon pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant enforce daily the submission of all the reports and documents from the Municipal Treasurer. All submitted reports and supporting documents shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month to the Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under par. 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through the bank, as a means of improving fiscal management in accordance with Section 4(4) and (8) of PD 1445, with the expected result of minimizing the attendant risks identified with cash-paid payrolls and easing the effects of manpower attrition;
Make a representation/arrange with any government bank like the LBP for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by the employees through a Memorandum of Agreement; and
Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement to strengthen fiscal management.

We recommended that the LDRRMO and Accounting Office prioritize the preparation and submission of the reports on LDRRMF to aid stakeholders/management in performing their decision making functions. The reports should contain complete and relevant information.

We recommended that the management:
Make representation to PCSO and DILG with the documents of donated motor vehicles;
Follow-up the suppliers of the documents of all purchased motor vehicles; and
Exert extra effort to have the vehicles properly registered with the LTO under the name of LGU.

We recommended that the management submit to this office the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly reconciled with the accounting records for CY 2016 and the years thereafter.

We recommended that the Management:
Be realistic and economical in planning the work to be done  in the projects to be implemented to avoid the occurrence of the above mentioned deficiencies;
Follow strictly the documentary requirements as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and fill up all the required data properly; and
Refund the deficiencies in the total amount of P 26,739.97 and submit to the Office of the Audit Team the duly authenticated machine copy of the Official Receipt.

We recommended that the Management:
Modify/correct the defects immediately and submit the duly dated and authenticated pictures (before, during and after) to the office of the audit team;
Strictly follow the provisions of the Municipal Ordinance No. 21 series of 2013 in the implementation of project in the coastal zone and Sections 62.2 of the RIRR of RA 9184 in correcting the defects; and
Justify the above findings.

We recommend that management should set timeline to other departments for early submission of financial records to the accounting unit. The Municipal Accountant shall then prepare the bank reconciliation statement every month as provided in the New Government Accounting System Manual and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
We recommend that management should require the Accountant to submit the monthly accounts to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.

We recommend the following:

Management should require the budget committee during the preparation of annual budget for 20% DF to include only P/P/A/s which are feasible, and could be completed as to target date of execution, after taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership. Likewise, there should be regular monitoring for timely and efficient implementation of these projects.

Management should consider the impact of the project in prioritizing its implementation.
